metadata.annotation_scheme: PLT (Post-Lingua Trace) + Semantic Fields + TraceLog Ontology
metadata.domain: Ziyarat tourism / Islamic pilgrimage / Sacred heritage
metadata.theoretical_framework: Fractal Metascience Paradigm (FMP): NULLO, PLT, UCOMM, EUO
metadata.expansion_rationale: 17 languages selected on basis of Uzbekistan inbound tourism statistics 2023 (UzStat), UN official languages, Islamic world coverage, and Central Asian regional languages
metadata.language_selection_source: Agentstvo statistiki RUz (2023): Tajikistan 2.15M, Kyrgyzstan 1.75M, Kazakhstan 1.33M, Russia 714K, Turkey 106.5K, Turkmenistan 100.3K, India 45.5K, China 42.5K, South Korea 37.1K, Germany 29.6K + UN official (EN/FR/ES/AR/ZH/RU)
metadata.expanded_by: Tillyabayeva Gulsum B. + Abdukarimov Abdurashid A.
metadata.publication_basis: Tillyabayeva G.B., Abdukarimov A.A. «Ўзбек тилида зиёрат туризми терминологиясини унификациялаш муаммолари». 2026.

terms.tracelog_function: Primary communication protocol between living and sacred past
terms.plt_note: Transmitted through ritual movement — gesture, route, approach direction — as much as through verbal instruction. Classic PLT carrier.
contexts: «Зиёрат қилдим» — I performed ziyarat (standard verbal formula)
«Зиёрат нияти» — intention of ziyarat (threshold PLT phrase)
«Чилла зиёрати» — forty-day mazar stay (extreme PLT practice)

etymology.root: Arabic ز-و-ر (z-w-r) 'to visit, to turn aside'
etymology.arabic_form: زيارة (ziyāra) — verbal noun of Form III
etymology.uzbek_entry: Entered Uzbek via Classical Persian and Chagatai literary tradition
etymology.turkic_parallel: No native Turkic equivalent — Arabic term fully adopted
etymology.semantic_development: Neutral 'visit' → specialized 'sacred visit' in Islamic context

terms.standard_definition: Муқаддас шахс мозори, диний аҳамиятга эга маконга ибодат ёки эҳтиром мақсадида қилинадиган ташриф
terms.definition_source: Чориев З. Тарих атамаларининг қисқача изоҳли луғати. Тошкент, 1999.

terms.tracelog_function: Biological trace node — body of saint as physical anchor of civilizational memory
terms.plt_note: Mazar transmits PLT through tactile ritual: kissing threshold, touching tomb fabric, tying cloth. Body knowledge without text.
terms.women_note: Mazar is primary site of women's independent ritual practice. Soviet period: women maintained mazar tradition when mosques were closed — NULLO TraceLog survival.
contexts: «Мазорга бордим» — I went to the mazar
«Мазор тупроғи» — soil of the mazar (baraka carrier)
«Мазор атрофида айландим» — I circled the mazar (tawaf-parallel ritual)

etymology.arabic_form: مزار (mazār) — place noun (maf'al form), 'place of visiting'

terms.standard_definition: Авлиё ёки муқаддас шахс дафн этилган жой; кўпинча меъморий иншоот билан безатилган. Суфийлик анъанасида марказий зиёрат объекти.
terms.definition_source: Абашин С. Мазар Бобои-об. МАЭ РАН, 2010; Чориев З. 1999.

terms.tracelog_function: Human TraceLog node — saint's body, life and teaching as civilizational trace anchor
terms.plt_note: Avliyo's karamat (miracles) transmitted through oral narrative — stories persist without institutional support. Classic NULLO survival mechanism.
terms.women_note: Female saints (avliyo ayollar) documented but understudied. Key figures: Bibi Seshanba, Fatima Zohra shrines. Women as primary transmitters of avliyo oral tradition.
contexts: «Авлиё руҳи» — spirit of the saint (baraka source)
«Авлиёнинг карамати» — miracle of the saint (PLT narrative)
«Авлиёлар мозори» — tombs of the saints (plural sacred complex)

etymology.root: Arabic و-ل-ي (w-l-y) 'to be near, to be a friend'
etymology.quranic_basis: Quran 10:62: 'Indeed, the allies of Allah — there will be no fear concerning them'
etymology.uzbek_usage: Used primarily in singular 'avliyo'; plural form 'avliyolar' in vernacular

terms.standard_definition: Ислом динида Оллоҳга яқин киши; хомий, «бандаларнинг хомийси» маъноларини билдиради. Авлиё илоҳий қудратга эга, одамларнинг ҳаётига таъсир кўрсата оладиган киши ҳисобланган.
terms.definition_source: Чориев З. Тарих атамаларининг қисқача изоҳли луғати. Тошкент, 1999. – 95-б.

terms.tracelog_function: Primary NULLO-PLT operator — maintained ziyarat tradition under Soviet ban with zero institutional resources
terms.plt_note: Otin-oyi IS the living PLT: transmits ritual knowledge through direct embodied practice, not text. Body, gesture, voice = PLT channel. Zero budget, zero permission, zero institution — yet tradition survived.
terms.women_note: Central figure in НЕЗРИМАЯ НИТЬ project and Tillyabayeva dissertation. Soviet period documentation: Lauer (2008). Primary agent of NULLO-PLT tradition survival.

empirical_data.interview_type: deep interviews with otin-oyi and women ziyarat organizers
empirical_data.key_utterance_uzbek: «Биз чилла пайтида ҳечнарса кўтармаймиз, фақат ниятимизни кўтарамиз»
empirical_data.key_utterance_english: During chilla we carry nothing — we carry only our intention
empirical_data.fmp_reading: Zero material (NULLO) + intention as PLT channel = FMP NULLO-PLT mechanism in pure form

contexts: «Отин-ойи келади» — the otin-oyi is coming (community announcement)
«Отин-ойига бордим» — I went to the otin-oyi (religious instruction visit)
«Отин ўқиди» — the otin recited (Quran recitation at mazar)

etymology.root: otin: Old Turkic ötün/ödün 'woman, lady' (feminine honorific) + oyi: 'mother, respected woman'
etymology.regional_forms: otin (Fergana Valley, dominant), bibi (Bukhara region), oy-bibi (mixed)
etymology.historical_attestation: Attested from at least 15th century in Timurid sources
etymology.soviet_transformation: Role expanded during Soviet period as formal male religious structure suppressed — NULLO activation

terms.standard_definition: Қуръон ва диний илмларни ўқитувчи аёл; зиёрат маросимларини ва аёллар диний ҳаётини ташкил этувчи жамоа раҳнамоси.
terms.definition_source: Lauer C. Otin-Oyi in Soviet Uzbekistan // Central Asian Survey. 2008. Vol. 27(3). P. 215–228.

terms.tracelog_function: Sacred communication carrier — baraka flows between saint, site, and pilgrim as UCOMM signal
terms.plt_note: Baraka transmitted through touch, proximity, movement — not through words. Pure PLT transmission. Touching tomb fabric = baraka transfer.
contexts: «Барака олдим» — I received baraka (standard ziyarat outcome)
«Барака тупроғи» — blessed soil of mazar
«Баракали жой» — blessed place (highest honorific for sacred site)

etymology.root: Arabic ب-ر-ك (b-r-k) 'to kneel, to be blessed, to be abundant'
etymology.quranic_significance: Allah's barakah mentioned 32 times in Quran
etymology.sufi_development: Extended to mean spiritual power concentrated in saints and sacred objects, transmissible by proximity

terms.standard_definition: Оллоҳнинг инъоми ва марҳамати; авлиёлар ва муқаддас маконларда мужассамлашган илоҳий куч; зиёрат орқали олинадиган маънавий қувват.
terms.definition_source: Муминов А. Святые места Мавераннахра. Тошкент, 2005.

terms.tracelog_function: NULLO practice — maximum spiritual effect from zero material resources; body as sole instrument
terms.plt_note: Chilla at mazar: woman stays 40 days without leaving. Body presence at TraceLog node = PLT activation. Transmission through embodied duration, not text.
terms.women_note: Primary women's practice at mazars. Soviet period: chilla continued covertly — pure NULLO. Otin-oyi utterance: 'we carry only our intention'.
contexts: «Чилла ўтирдим» — I sat chilla (common expression for 40-day retreat)
«Чилла нияти» — chilla intention (set on entry to mazar)
«Чиллаи аёл» — women's chilla (specifically female practice variety)

etymology.pre_islamic_layer: 40-day cycle predates Islam in Central Asian ritual — Buddhist and shamanic parallels documented
etymology.sufi_formalization: Formalized by early Sufi masters; 40 days = spiritual transformation cycle

terms.standard_definition: Зиёратгоҳда ёки алоҳида маконда қирқ кун мобайнида қилинадиган маънавий чекиниш; аёллар зиёрат амалиётининг марказий маросими.
terms.definition_source: Tashkent field data (Tillyabayeva G.B., 2023-2025); Baldick J. Mystical Islam. 1989.

terms.tracelog_function: EUO node — self-organizing Sufi network center, functions without state apparatus
contexts: «Хонақоҳга кирдим» — I entered the khanqah (formal Sufi space entry)
«Хонақоҳда зикр» — dhikr in the khanqah (ritual remembrance)

etymology.historical_function: Center for Sufi teaching, pilgrimage rest-stop, social welfare. Often waqf-funded.

terms.standard_definition: Суфийлик тариқати марказ биноси; зикр, таълим ва зиёратчиларни қабул қилиш учун мўлжалланган иншоот.
terms.definition_source: Чориев З. Тарих атамаларининг қисқача изоҳли луғати. Тошкент, 1999.

terms.tracelog_function: Legal-economic TraceLog mechanism — maintains sacred sites through perpetual endowment
terms.uzbekistan_history: Waqf supported all major Uzbek shrines until Soviet confiscation (1927). Post-1991 partial restoration.
contexts: «Вақф ери» — waqf land (sacred endowment property)
«Вақф ҳужжати» — waqf document (legal deed)

etymology.root: Arabic و-ق-ف (w-q-f) 'to stop, to stand, to dedicate'
etymology.legal_form: Property 'stopped' from sale or inheritance — dedicated to God in perpetuity

terms.standard_definition: Диний ёки ижтимоий мақсадларга бағишланган ва муомаладан чиқарилган мулк; зиёратгоҳларни молиялаштиришнинг асосий ислом ҳуқуқий механизми.
terms.definition_source: Чориев З. Тарих атамаларининг қисқача изоҳли луғати. Тошкент, 1999.

terms.tracelog_function: EUO operationalization — institutional framework for activating TraceLog nodes for tourism
terms.state_definition: Зиёратгоҳ, мазор ёки диний аҳамиятга эга тарихий объектларга уюштирилган туристик фаолият
terms.uzbekistan_2030_target: 3 million pilgrimage tourists annually by 2030 (Uzbekiston-2030 Strategy)
terms.standard_definition: Зиёратгоҳ, мазор ёки диний аҳамиятга эга тарихий объектларга уюштирилган туристик фаолият; «Ўзбекистон–2030» стратегиясининг устувор йўналиши.

terms.tracelog_function: Theoretical core — describes transmission of meaning through gesture, route, ritual, material object rather than language
terms.definition_en: Post-Lingua Trace (PLT): the class of meaning-transmission mechanisms that operate below or beyond linguistic encoding — through embodied practice, spatial navigation, tactile ritual, material form, or silence.
terms.ziyarat_application: In ziyarat: tying cloth, walking barefoot, approach direction, touching threshold, circling tomb — all PLT, not verbal instruction
terms.otin_oyi_application: Otin-oyi's teaching method is PLT — demonstration, presence, repetition, not textbook
terms.sufi_application: Silsila transmission master-to-disciple: initiated through gaze, touch, breath — not written text
terms.primary_source: Abdukarimov A. Fractal Metascience Paradigm. SSRN, 2025; Invisible Shadow. Zenodo, 2025.
terms.standard_definition: Тил ёки матн орқали эмас, балки ҳаракат, маросим, маршрут ва материал объект орқали узатиладиган маъно; зиёрат амалиётида «тилдан кейинги из» марказий трансмиссия механизмидир.
terms.definition_source: Abdukarimov A. Invisible Shadow: Towards a Universal Ontology of Trace, Continuity, and Care. Zenodo, 2025.

translation_notes.uzbek: Primary corpus language. Both Cyrillic (academic/legal standard) and Latin (digital/youth generation) scripts provided throughout.
translation_notes.russian: Second language of all academic literature on Central Asian ziyarat. All major monographs available in Russian.
translation_notes.english: International scholarly language. UNWTO Religious Tourism Report (2019) standard terms used.
translation_notes.german: Key partner language — DAAD / VolkswagenStiftung / Horizon Europe cooperation context.
translation_notes.tajik: Closest living language to Uzbek with shared ziyarat tradition. 2.15M tourists (2023) — largest non-CIS group.
translation_notes.kyrgyz: Second largest tourist source (1.75M, 2023). Shared Central Asian ziyarat and Manas oral tradition.
translation_notes.kazakh: Third largest source (1.33M, 2023). Shared Sufi tradition (Yasawi order, Turkestan shrine).
translation_notes.turkish: Fifth source (106.5K, 2023). Rich Ottoman-Sufi terminology (tekke, türbe, vakıf).
translation_notes.persian: Classical language of all Uzbek Islamic scholarship. All major Sufi texts originally in Persian.
translation_notes.arabic: Sacred language of Islam. All religious terms traceable to Arabic roots. UN official language.
translation_notes.pashto: Included for Islamic scholarly context — Afghanistan shares Naqshbandi tradition and ziyarat practice.
translation_notes.methodology: Translations based on: (1) Historical dictionaries (Choriyev 1999, Rakhmatullayev 1978, Geografiyadagi izohli lugat 1979), (2) State documents lex.uz (2019-2026), (3) Field observations Tillyabayeva (2023-2025), (4) Classical Sufi terminology glossaries, (5) uzbekcorpora.uz national corpus

sources: Sufi terminology dictionaries (Hujwiri Kashf al-Mahjub, Ghazali Ihya)

plt_statistics.semantic_preservation_principle: Multiple translations capture different semantic nuances rather than forcing single 'correct' equivalent — direct continuation of navoiy-terra-corpus v1.1 methodology
